summary: Iteration 3 (code-quality refactor) regression testing complete. Backend: 31/32 pytest tests passed across both existing test files. Frontend: all 5 smoke-tested flows passed (login, sessionStorage token, refresh persistence, lead drawer + activity CRUD, inventory sorted grid + status cycle) with ZERO console warnings/errors. Refactor introduced no behavior changes.

minor.issue: test_devam_crm.py::TestInventory::test_list_filter asserts exactly 80 units in Shilp Aaria, but receives 84 — this is test-pollution from test_devam_iter2.py::TestInventoryBulk::test_bulk_create which posts 4 new units to the SAME project (some_project fixture returns projects[0] = Shilp Aaria). Pre-existing brittleness; NOT a regression from this iteration. Fix: use >= 80 OR scope bulk_create to a TEST_ prefixed project.

action_items: (Optional) Fix brittle assertion in test_devam_crm.py::TestInventory::test_list_filter — change `assert len(units) == 80` to `assert len(units) >= 80` to tolerate iter2 bulk-create test pollution, OR have test_bulk_create use a dedicated TEST_ project (it already creates TEST_BulkProj_* projects but seems to write into Shilp Aaria — re-check fixture `some_project`).

(Pre-existing, unrelated to this iteration) server.py CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True — browsers reject this combo for cookie-based auth. Move to explicit origin list before production.
(Pre-existing) server.py is now 974 lines — split into routers (auth, leads, projects, inventory, users) before next feature iteration.
critical_code_review_comments: seed_data orchestrator now nicely split into 7 _seed_* helpers (admin, builders, projects, inventory, leads, visits, bookings) — clean. _seed_admin_user runs every startup (idempotent), demo data gated by `db.builders.count_documents == 0` (skipped on warm DB) — same gating as before.
score_lead_with_ai now delegates to _parse_ai_score_reply + _fallback_rule_score — both helpers return the same shape {score, category} so swap is transparent.

AuthContext.jsx uses sessionStorage (verified at runtime — key `devam_token` present in sessionStorage, absent in localStorage). useMemo wraps the context value. Token survives page reload within the tab via http-only refresh cookie + /auth/me on mount.

Inventory.jsx groupedSorted memo: visually verified order — Tower A→B ascending, Floors 5→1 descending, Units 501→504 ascending in each row. No console warnings about missing memo deps or duplicate keys.
Lead drawer activity flow end-to-end works: open drawer → fill activity-content-input → click activity-submit → note appears in timeline. No regression.

success_rate.backend: 96.9% (31/32) — single failure is pre-existing test pollution, not a refactor regression
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, sessionStorage migration, refresh persistence, leads drawer + activity, inventory sorted grid + status cycle)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created by this iteration. Pre-existing TEST_ prefixed leftovers from prior iterations remain in DB (a TEST_BulkProj_* project, TEST_iter3_smoke_note activity on first lead). Not blocking.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 is a pure refactor — no behavior changes verified. Backend pytest suite 31/32 (single failure is brittle assertion, not regression). Frontend smoke fully green. sessionStorage key is `devam_token`. Login submits to /api/auth/login and routes to /app. Leads at /app/leads (data-testid lead-row-* per row), Inventory at /app/inventory (data-testid unit-<tower>-<floor><unit>). Activity testids: activity-content-input, activity-submit. Login testids: login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-button.
rca of the issue: test_list_filter failure RCA — Reproduction: run iter2 bulk_create test before iter1 inventory list_filter test. iter2 fixture `some_project` returns projects[0] which is the seeded 'Shilp Aaria' (80 units). bulk_create posts 4 more units (1 tower, 2 floors, 2 units = 4) targeting that same project, so the list endpoint now returns 84. Pytest test ordering currently has iter1 list_filter run AFTER iter2 patch tests in same session, but bulk_create runs even later — however when reusing a warm DB from prior runs, the 84 leak persists. Mitigation options: (1) make assertion `>= 80`, (2) point bulk_create to a TEST_-prefixed dedicated project, or (3) add an autouse teardown that deletes inventory units created mid-test. Marked minor; no fix applied to avoid scope-creep on a refactor-only iteration.
